KeysToEntries.Belt_LeatherBelt_Description: A leather belt with a clip case.
KeysToEntries.Belt_CondorTacticalBelt_Description: A rugged and functional belt designed for tactical and outdoor use. Made from durable materials, it provides a reliable platform for carrying essential gear and accessories.
KeysToEntries.Belt_CustomGearLowproBelt_Description: A low-profile tactical belt with a smaller 25mm Cobra buckle. Made in the Czech Republic, this belt is fully MOLLE-compatible, and thanks to its versatility, it is also perfect for everyday use.
KeysToEntries.Belt_LightweightCombatBelt_Description: This tactical belt features a breathable mesh base with three rows of MOLLE, plus an internal waist belt for fastening and size adjustment
KeysToEntries.Belt_CustomGearMc50LaserEdition_Descript: A feature-rich tactical belt, designed to meet the demands of professionals in military and law enforcement. Made in the Czech Republic, this belt features a 50mm Cobra buckle and is MOLLE-compatible.
KeysToEntries.Belt_CivilianSportFannyPack_Description: A small bag worn around the waist. It offers limited storage space and may not be suitable for carrying larger items or valuables securely.
KeysToEntries.Belt_CivilianLeatherFannyPack_Descriptio: A small bag worn around the waist. Made from genuine leather, it is durable but offers limited storage space and may not be suitable for carrying larger items or valuables securely.
KeysToEntries.Belt_RussianFannyPack_Description: A comfortable and secure belt bag with double-locked zippers and a Velcro panel.
KeysToEntries.Belt_LightAnatomicalBeltType2_Olive_Desc: A durable belt with a flat profile that adapts to its wearer's anatomy for increased comfort. Thanks to its modularity, it can be equipped with many pouches, hip panels, or holsters.
KeysToEntries.Belt_LightAnatomicalBeltType2_BossMB_Des:
A durable belt with a flat profile that adapts to its wearer's anatomy for increased comfort. Thanks to its modularity, it can be equipped with many pouches, hip panels, or holsters.

This belt belongs to Arkady "Buran" Vititsky, the military commander of Fort Narith.
